task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz interface is visible with the 'dwanko_run' scene loaded, verifiable by the scene name in the title bar, xsheet, or timeline.

task_completion.description: Access the FX schematic or FX settings to apply an effect.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FX Schematic window or FX Settings dialog is open and visible in the OpenToonz interface.

task_completion.description: Apply a Wave, Ripple, or similar distortion FX to the character level.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An FX node labeled 'Wave', 'Ripple', or a similar distortion effect is visible in the FX schematic and is connected to the character level/column.

task_completion.description: Set the output directory to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/underwater_fx/' and the format to PNG.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings window is open, showing the 'Save In' path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/underwater_fx' (or similar relative path) and the file format set to 'png'.

task_completion.description: Render the first 24 frames of the animation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar or render window is visible in OpenToonz, showing the frames being processed up to at least frame 24.

task_completion.description: Verify the final rendered output shows the character with an underwater distortion effect.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendered frame, preview window, or image viewer shows the 'dwanko' character with a clear wavy, rippled, or distorted visual effect applied, simulating looking through water.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to apply the effect and render the animation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the OpenToonz graphical interface (e.g., clicking menus, adjusting nodes, opening output settings) rather than using terminal commands to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent applied the distortion effect within OpenToonz.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz FX schematic shows the distortion node actively connected to the character level, proving the effect was generated in-app rather than editing images externally.

integrity.description: The agent used OpenToonz's render function to generate the output files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz render window or progress bar is visible, rather than terminal commands copying or moving pre-existing files to the output directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct scene path is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz'. The required output directory is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/underwater_fx/' and the format must be 'png'. The agent must render at least 24 frames. The final output must visually demonstrate a wavy or distorted effect applied to the character to simulate an underwater environment.
